tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, mild ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with abnormal urine appearance and color, but no evidence of infection. HbA1c is unexpectedly low despite glucosuria, possibly due to anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. Recommend further renal evaluation and assessment for possible underlying metabolic or hematologic conditions.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
